title: callistemon serpentinus
intro: Callistemon serpentinus, also known as the serpent ironbark, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is native to Western Australia and has been used by indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Callistemon serpentinus include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of colds and flu. Callistemon serpentinus is traditionally prepared as a tea by boiling the leaves in water, and it can also be used in tinctures and salves for topical application.
